title: WOS Workflow
description: Single author-time schema for a WOS workflow. Scales from Forms+ intake (~30 lines) to complex case management (~3000 lines) without changing shape. Required core: impactLevel, actors, lifecycle. Optional embedded blocks (governance, agents, aiOversight, signature, custody, advanced, assurance) appear only when product behavior demands them. Conditional rules require governance for rights-impacting/safety-impacting workflows, agents block for agent-typed actors, and signature block for signature-gated transitions. Deployment-environment configuration (calendars, notification templates, ontology alignment) lives in two sidecars referenced by targetWorkflow URI. See ADR 0076 (merged-envelope consolidation) and ADR 0063 (embedded-vs-sidecar identity boundary).
$comment: Promotion status (PLN-0314): top-level core leaves carry full normative description + examples + x-lm.critical annotations on load-bearing nodes. Inner-block leaves under governance/agents/aiOversight/signature/custody/advanced/assurance remain sketch-quality pending the spec absorption pass (PLN-0176..0207); the canonical descriptions for those leaves are owned by kernel/spec.md, governance/spec.md, ai/ai-integration.md, and advanced/spec.md and absorb here once those §-numbered sections land. SCHEMA-DOC-001 is CI-gated at zero violations for the full workflow schema (including inner blocks); PLN-0176..0207 tracks richer normative prose cross-walk from specs into those nodes. The six canonical kernel extension seams (ADR 0077) — actorExtension, contractHook, provenanceLayer, lifecycleHook, custodyHook, extensions/x- keys — remain authoritative; new kernel extension points MUST attach to one of these or be ratified via a new ADR amending Kernel §10. Step 5 ($defs promotion) landed: FactsTierRecord, MutationSource, VerificationLevel, CaseFileSnapshot, CapabilityInvocationRecord live here (plus transitive ProvenanceOutcome); the runtime log schema (wos-provenance-log.schema.json) $refs them at https://wos-spec.org/schemas/wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/<Name> and layers runtime-record-shape allOf entries (CaseCreated/IntakeAccepted/IntakeRejected/IntakeDeferred/SignatureAffirmation) on top.

$wosWorkflow.description: Sole top-level version marker that identifies this object as a WOS Workflow Document and pins it to the 1.0 line of the merged-schema product-tier model (ADR 0076). Stream identity (governance, agents, signature, custody, advanced) is implicit in the envelope version; compliance claims compose as $wosWorkflow@X.Y. Replaces the historical four-document markers $wosKernel / $wosWorkflowGovernance / $wosAIIntegration / $wosAdvanced.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker and version pin for WOS Workflow documents per ADR 0076

wosVersionPin.description: Optional typed claims-map pinning this workflow to a specific WOS envelope version + the set of embedded blocks the authors intend to exercise. The compiler refuses to emit when the pinned envelopeVersion does not match its compiled-in SCHEMA_VERSION (per Studio compiler-contract SA-MUST-cmp-052). Replaces the previous free-form claims sentence in RELEASE-STREAMS.md with a typed shape that tooling can reason about.

url.description: Canonical URI identifier for this Workflow Document. The URL names the workflow definition, not any particular instance — all versions of the same workflow SHOULD share this URL, and the pair (url, version) SHOULD be globally unique. Sidecars (`wos-delivery`, `wos-ontology-alignment`) join by this URL via `targetWorkflow`. Prefer stable, resolvable URIs under a namespace the authoring organization controls.

x-lm.intent: Stable identifier of the workflow across versions; sidecars join here

version.description: Version string for this Workflow Document. Semantic Versioning (SemVer 2.0.0) is RECOMMENDED so processors and governance layers can reason about compatibility. Instance versioning (Kernel §9.6) pins each running workflow instance to the (url, version) pair of the definition that created it.

title.description: Short human-readable name for this workflow, suitable for lists, navigation, and audit summaries. Not interpreted by the processor.

description.description: Free-text description of this workflow's purpose, scope, and intended audience. Consumed by documentation tooling and LLM authoring agents; not interpreted by the processor.
examples: Internal workflow for approving purchase orders under $50k; routed to a manager for approval or rejection.
Medicaid eligibility redetermination with appeal branch; rights-impacting, requires full due process.

status.description: Publication status of this workflow definition. `draft` means the definition is being authored and SHOULD NOT instantiate production cases. `active` means the definition is in effect and new instances MAY be created. `deprecated` means no new instances SHOULD be created, though existing pinned instances continue executing.

impactLevel.description: Consequence level classification for decisions made within this workflow (Kernel §6). `rights-impacting`: decisions affect individual legal rights — full due process REQUIRED (governance block MUST be present). `safety-impacting`: decisions affect safety — full due process REQUIRED (governance block MUST be present). `operational`: organizational operations — governance RECOMMENDED. `informational`: no binding decisions — governance OPTIONAL. Drives conditional block requirements via the schema's allOf rules.

x-lm.intent: Proportionality index that governs the strength of required controls across all embedded blocks

caseFile.description: Structured case data for instances of this workflow: either inline `fields` (FieldDeclaration map) or a pinned `contractRef` (Formspec Definition recommended). Mutually exclusive shapes per CaseFile oneOf.

x-lm.intent: Structured case data shape — either inline field declaration or Formspec contract reference

actors.description: Non-empty list of actors (humans, systems, or agents) that may perform transitions. Each entry has a unique `id`; transitions reference these ids. When any actor uses type `agent`, the `agents` and `aiOversight` blocks are required (lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001).

x-lm.intent: Who can act in this workflow — humans, systems, or agents (the agent type is gated by AI Integration block presence per ADR 0076 D-2; cross-ref enforced by lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001)

lifecycle.description: Author-time state machine: `initialState` plus a map of state identifiers to State declarations (atomic, compound, parallel, foreach, or final). Topology is immutable for a pinned (url, version); runtime instances follow this graph.

x-lm.intent: State machine spine — initial state + lifecycle topology that all other concerns attach to

governance.description: Embedded Layer-1 governance (due process, review protocols, validation pipelines, policy parameters) inside the author-time workflow envelope. Required for rights-impacting and safety-impacting impactLevel per kernel and ADR 0076 D-2.

x-lm.intent: Embedded due-process / review / pipeline governance — REQUIRED for rights-impacting/safety-impacting workflows per ADR 0076 D-2

agents.description: Per-agent declarations (model, autonomy, deontic constraints, drift policy) embedded in the merged workflow. Required when any actor has type agent (lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001).

x-lm.intent: Per-agent declarations — REQUIRED when any actor has type='agent' (lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001 enforces the cross-reference)

aiOversight.description: Cross-cutting AI oversight embedded in the workflow envelope: disclosure posture, drift detection, narrative tier handling, volume constraints. Pairs with the agents array (ADR 0076).

x-lm.intent: Cross-cutting AI policy — disclosure (EU AI Act Art. 13, OMB M-24-10), drift detection, narrative tier; pairs with agents block

signature.description: Embedded DocuSign-tier signature profile (roles, documents, signingFlow, evidence) inside the author-time envelope. Required when transitions gate on kind signature (lint WOS-SIG-COVER-001).

x-lm.intent: Embedded signature workflow — LOAD-BEARING for DocuSign-tier (signing order IS the workflow); REQUIRED when any transition gates on kind='signature' (lint WOS-SIG-COVER-001 enforces signer coverage)

custody.description: Embedded Trellis custody binding (trust profile, anchor requirements) for workflows that claim anchoring on transitions or signature events.

x-lm.intent: Trellis custody binding — load-bearing whenever a workflow claims anchoring on transitions or signature events

decisionTables.description: Top-level decision-table catalog per Kernel §4.5.1. Each entry is a structured table referenceable by a transition `guard` of form `{kind: "decisionTable", ref, outputColumn, inputBindings}`. Tables compose with the kernel's lifecycle (§4.7) and FEL evaluator: input bindings evaluate in the transition's full context; row input/output cells evaluate in a row scope where declared inputs are bound. Hit policies (`unique`/`first`/`priority`/`collect`) determine row selection. `collect` is reserved for non-guard consumers; transition-guard tables MUST select a boolean output column. Authoring round-trip is structurally lossless — a Studio DecisionTable PolicyObject projects to one entry here without flattening to chained-FEL.

rows.rationale: 1-person household, monthly gross under federal SNAP threshold.
Catch-all; income exceeds threshold or household-size unhandled.

x-lm.intent: Tabular decision logic as a first-class kernel construct; FEL-native (not DMN); referenced from transition guards via DecisionTableGuard

extensions.description: Vendor-namespaced extension container realizing Kernel §10.6 (the `extensions` seam — one of the canonical six per ADR 0077). Every key MUST start with `x-`; the prefix `x-wos-` is RESERVED for future normative WOS use (lint K-EXT-002 warns). Values are opaque to the processor; vendors document their own subkeys.

$schema.description: Standard JSON Schema annotation declaring the meta-schema URI. Not interpreted by the WOS processor; consumed by IDEs/validators for tooling integration.

execution.description: Execution configuration (Kernel §9). Workflow/task/service timeouts, compensability, and instance-versioning policy. Honored by the durable runtime adapter; spec-authoritative semantics live in `wos-runtime`.

workflowTimeout.description: Maximum total workflow duration as an ISO 8601 duration. The durable runtime treats this as the outer case-level timeout.

defaultTaskTimeout.description: Default task timeout as an ISO 8601 duration when an individual task omits an explicit SLA or timeout override.

defaultServiceTimeout.description: Default service-call timeout as an ISO 8601 duration when an invocation does not declare a narrower timeout.

compensable.description: Whether the workflow scope is compensable under Kernel §9.5, meaning failed side effects require declared compensation behavior.

instanceVersioning.description: Instance versioning policy (Kernel §9.6). `pinned` keeps each running instance bound to the (url, version) of the Kernel Document that created it. `migrateable` permits explicit migration to a newer compatible revision when the runtime records the migration. Omitted policy MUST be treated as `pinned` (spec §1483).

evaluationMode.description: Evaluation mode for guards and computed expressions (Kernel §12). `event-driven` (default) re-evaluates on transition events; `continuous` re-evaluates on any case-file mutation.

maxRelationshipEventDepth.description: Cascade depth cap for `$related.*` events under Kernel §4.10. Zero disables relationship-event expansion.

allOf.title: Rights-impacting and safety-impacting workflows MUST embed governance.
Workflows whose actors include any agent-typed actor MUST declare agents[].

allOf.$comment: Cross-reference rule beyond JSON Schema expressivity: every actor with type=='agent' MUST have a matching agents[].id. Enforced by lint rule WOS-AGENT-XREF-001.

Cross-cutting rule beyond JSON Schema expressivity: any transition gated by signature (on.kind=='signature') requires a signature block, and signers[] MUST cover the gating actor. Enforced by lint rule WOS-SIG-COVER-001.

allOf.title: Workflows with both agents and advanced features SHOULD declare aiOversight.
Embedded blocks MUST NOT declare targetWorkflow (ADR 0063 §2.1).
allOf.$comment: The merged $wosWorkflow envelope is the sole author-time identity boundary. Embedded blocks govern the enclosing workflow; they do not target other workflows. Only sidecars (wos-delivery, wos-ontology-alignment) target workflows by URI. Lint WOS-EMBED-TARGET-001.

allOf.title: Embedded blocks MUST NOT declare independent url or version (ADR 0063 §2.1).
allOf.$comment: The enclosing $wosWorkflow envelope's url and version are the sole identity. Embedded blocks have no independent identity. Lint WOS-EMBED-IDENTITY-001.

allOf.title: Rights-impacting and safety-impacting workflows MUST embed custody (ADR 0076).
allOf.$comment: Mirrors the governance and signature conditionals: any workflow whose impactLevel triggers full due process MUST also bind a custody surface (Trellis trust-profile + per-transition or per-signature-event anchor requirements + export-bundle reference). Previously delegated to lint; promoted to schema 2026-05-02 so violations halt at JSON-Schema validation rather than waiting for downstream lint passes.

ImpactLevel.$comment: Name-promoted alias of `properties/impactLevel` (Kernel §6 proportionality index). Lifted to `$defs` so consumers — most notably the public API projection in `schemas/api/task.schema.json#/$defs/ImpactLevel` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6) — can `$ref` a stable target instead of pointing at the inline `properties` path. Closed enum; the document-level `properties/impactLevel` keeps the inline form for required-key bookkeeping at the root and remains canonical for human authors.

InstanceStatus.$comment: Name-promoted alias of the runtime instance-status enum, originally inlined at `wos-process.schema.json#/properties/status` (ADR 0070 D-5 — `stalled` reframed as a reserved `InstanceStatus` variant). Lifted to `$defs` so the public API projection in `schemas/api/instance.schema.json#/$defs/LifecycleState` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6) and any future consumer can `$ref` a stable target. Closed enum: `active` accepts events; `suspended` is paused; `migrating` is mid-version-change; `completed`/`terminated` are irreversible terminal states; `stalled` exhausted the commit retry budget and is recoverable via operator intervention.

CaseFile.description: Structured case data. Either declared inline via fields, or referenced via contractRef. Formspec is the recommended contract binding (kernel contractHook seam).

fields.description: Inline field declarations for the case data shape. Each entry's key is the field name and value is a `FieldDeclaration` describing type and constraints. Use `fields` for self-contained workflows; use `contractRef` to reference an external contract (Formspec recommended).

contractRef.description: URI reference to an external contract document defining the case data shape. Recommended binding is a Formspec Definition (per the canonical `contractHook` seam, ADR 0077 §10.2); JSON Schema is the baseline binding. Mutually exclusive with inline `fields`.

contractVersion.description: Optional version string pin for the referenced contract. When omitted, processors resolve the latest published version of `contractRef`. Pinning is RECOMMENDED for workflow processes that must replay against archived semantics (Kernel §9.6).

required.description: When true, the field MUST be present and non-null at validation time. When omitted or false, the field is optional. Enforcement layer is the contract validator bound by `contractHook`.

description.description: Free-text description of the field's purpose, displayed to authoring tools and rendered into LLM-readable artifacts. Not interpreted by the processor; the field's semantic meaning lives in the bound contract.
examples: The applicant's full legal name as it appears on government-issued ID.
Monthly gross income in USD, before taxes.

canonicalTermRef.description: IRI reference to a canonical-vocabulary term that defines this field's semantic meaning across workflows (Studio terminology-and-canonical-vocabulary spec). Lets governance reviewers compare like-shaped fields across programs without ambiguity. Optional but RECOMMENDED for fields that re-occur across workflows (e.g. monthlyIncome, householdSize, applicantSSN).

dpvSensitivity.description: Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV) classification for the field's sensitivity. Drives downstream lint POM-LINT-DPV-001: when set to a special-category value (e.g. dpv:HealthData, dpv:BiometricData, dpv:SpecialCategoryData), the collecting EvidenceRequirement MUST carry a retentionPolicy. The kernel does not interpret the value beyond its presence; consuming tools and lint rules act on the IRI. Optional; absent means "no special handling required".

FieldDeclaration.description: Author-time field declaration in a contract intake binding (Kernel §9.1). Pairs a field name with its primitive type and optional documentation; the bound contract carries the semantic meaning. Optional canonicalTermRef + dpvSensitivity surfaces are extension seams for terminology alignment and privacy classification (added 2026-05-02 per Studio policy-object-model + terminology specs).

IntakeReference.description: Public-intake form. References a Formspec definition. Validated intake is converted to caseFile via IntakeHandoff (ADR 0073).

contractRef.description: URI reference to the Formspec Definition that defines the public-intake form. Validated intake responses are converted to `caseFile` via `IntakeHandoff` per ADR 0073. Required for any workflow that accepts public intake.

contractVersion.description: Optional version pin for the intake form contract. When omitted, processors resolve latest. Pinning is RECOMMENDED for SBA pilot deployments to keep production replay deterministic.

id.description: Stable identifier for this actor within the workflow document. MUST be unique across actors[]. Cross-references include `Transition.actor` (who acts on the transition) and, when type=='agent', `agents[].id`. Identifier convention: camelCase or kebab-case strings; no whitespace.

type.$comment: Canonical actor kinds: human, system, agent. Vendor-defined kinds use x- prefix.
type.description: Actor classification. Canonical values are `human`, `system`, and `agent`; vendor-defined values MUST use an `x-` prefixed identifier via the `actorExtension` seam (ADR 0077).

role.description: Optional human-readable role label for documentation and notice rendering. Not interpreted by the processor; the actor identity for execution and authority decisions is `id`, not `role`.

states.description: Map of state identifier to State declaration. Defines the workflow's complete topology: atomic states (one transition target per event), compound states (nested substates), parallel states (concurrent regions), foreach states (bounded iteration per ADR 0078), and final states.

initialState.description: Identifier of the lifecycle's initial state — the state instances enter on creation. MUST match a key in `states`. Per Kernel §4.1, every workflow has exactly one initial state.

items.description: Semantic tag string attaching governance and policy to this state. Governance attaches via the `lifecycleHook` seam (ADR 0077 §10.4) by matching tags. Common tags: `determination`, `adverse-decision`, `hold`, `appeal`, `terminal`. Custom tags MUST use kebab-case; vendor-specific tags use `x-` prefix.

states.description: Nested substates for compound states (`type=='compound'`). Map of substate identifier to State declaration. Compound states enter their declared `initial` substate; history states (Kernel §4.14) optionally restore the most recently active substate on re-entry.

regions.description: Parallel regions for parallel states (`type=='parallel'`). Map of region identifier to a region object containing its own `initial` and `states`. All regions execute concurrently; the parallel state exits only when join semantics declared by `cancellationPolicy` are satisfied (Kernel §4.8).

onEntry.description: Ordered list of actions the processor MUST execute on state entry, before any transitions are evaluated. Action shape follows Kernel §9.2 action execution model (invokeService, startTimer, emitEvent, etc.). Actions execute in declaration order; the state is considered entered only after all onEntry actions succeed.

onExit.description: Ordered list of actions the processor MUST execute on state exit, after the outgoing transition has been selected but before the next state is entered. Same action shape as onEntry. Exit actions cannot abort the transition; they execute as side effects.

stateTimeout.description: ISO 8601 duration after which the processor emits a `$timeout.state` event if the state has not been exited. The lifecycle handles the timeout like any other event (Kernel §4.10). Hold policies (Governance §12) MAY override stateTimeout with policy-specific durations.

collection.$comment: Foreach only. FEL expression yielding a bounded array. ADR 0078.
collection.description: Foreach states only (`type=='foreach'`). FEL expression yielding a bounded array. The processor evaluates the expression at state entry, instantiates `body` once per item, and aggregates per `mergeStrategy`. Per ADR 0078, the array MUST be bounded — unbounded collections are rejected.

itemVariable.description: Foreach states only. Variable name bound to the current item within `body`. Defaults to `$item`. Referenced in body actions and guards as `$item.fieldName` or whatever name is declared.

indexVariable.description: Foreach states only. Variable name bound to the current item index (0-based) within `body`. Defaults to `$index`. Useful for ordered processing or correlation with parallel arrays.

concurrency.description: Foreach states only. Maximum number of items processed concurrently. Positive integer for bounded concurrency; null for unbounded (processor decides). Zero is rejected (would deadlock; lint K-FOREACH-003 also enforces post-coercion). Processors MAY refuse unbounded concurrency on rights-impacting workflows.

breakCondition.description: Foreach states only. FEL expression evaluated after each iteration; when true, terminates the foreach early. Useful for early-exit on first match or threshold reached.

outputPath.description: Foreach states only. Case-file path where iteration results are written per `mergeStrategy`. Per ADR 0078, the write goes through the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080) with `mutationSource: computed`.

initialState.description: Identifier of the initial substate for compound states. MUST be a key in `states`. Required when `type=='compound'` (Kernel S4.3).

outcomeCode.description: Final states only (`type=='final'`). Stable code identifying the workflow's terminal outcome. Recorded in Facts-tier provenance and consumed by reporting / case-closure pipelines. Distinct from `tags` (which compose) — outcomeCode is singular.

allOf.title: ForEach states MUST declare collection + body (lint K-FOREACH-001 / K-FOREACH-002).

DecisionTable.description: A first-class decision table per Kernel §4.5.1. Inputs and outputs are typed; rows carry FEL boolean predicates per input cell and FEL value expressions per output cell. Document order is significant for `first` and `priority` hit policies. A row matches when every input cell evaluates to `true`; the hit policy resolves multi-match. Cell expressions evaluate in a row-only scope (the declared inputs, bound to the values the referencing DecisionTableGuard's `inputBindings` produced) — they MUST NOT reference `caseFile`, `$event`, or any outer namespace. Transition-guard tables MUST select a `boolean`-typed output column.

id.description: Unique identifier within the document. Referenced by DecisionTableGuard.ref. SHOULD be stable across versions so projection-source provenance (e.g., a Studio DecisionTable PolicyObject) remains traceable.

description.description: Human-readable prose explaining why this table exists, what jurisdiction or program it serves, and how reviewers should interpret its rows. Consumed by authoring UIs and audit bundles; not evaluated by the runtime.
examples: SNAP gross-income test for household size brackets (FFY26).
Medicaid MAGI household composition gate before financial tests.

inputs.description: Typed input parameters of the table. Each input is bound by the referencing DecisionTableGuard's `inputBindings` and exposed inside row cell expressions under its declared `name`.

name.description: Input identifier token bound into the row evaluation scope; MUST match `^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$` and appear identically inside row cell FEL expressions.

type.description: FEL type of the input value. Cell expressions reading this input MUST be type-compatible.

description.description: Optional prose for reviewers and LLM authors describing how this input is sourced, normalized, or constrained before row evaluation.
examples: Monthly gross income in USD before taxes, aligned to contract field monthlyIncome.
Countable household members excluding roomers who do not purchase and prepare food together.

outputs.description: Typed output columns. A DecisionTableGuard selects exactly one column by name (`outputColumn`); for transition-guard tables that column MUST be `boolean`-typed.

name.description: Output column identifier; referenced by DecisionTableGuard.outputColumn.

type.description: FEL type of the produced output value. Output cell expressions MUST evaluate to this type.

description.description: Optional prose describing how downstream consumers should interpret this output column, including units and rounding expectations where relevant.
examples: Boolean guard result consumed by transition resolution.
Numeric copay tier index where 0 means exempt.

rows.description: Decision rows in document order. Each row carries one FEL boolean predicate per declared input and one FEL value expression per declared output.

hitPolicy.description: Row-selection rule when zero, one, or many rows match. `unique`: at most one match (>1 → K-052); `first`: first match in document order wins; `priority`: lowest `priority` integer among matches wins (ties → K-052); `collect`: all matches returned to the caller (transition guards reject `collect` per K-053). Authoring SHOULD pick the most restrictive policy that fits — `unique` proves disjointness; `first` documents intentional ordering.

x-lm.intent: First-class kernel decision table; FEL-native (no DMN); referenced by DecisionTableGuard from transition guards

DecisionTableRow.description: One row of a DecisionTable. Matches when every entry of `inputCells` evaluates to `true` against the row scope (the DecisionTable's declared inputs bound to the referencing guard's `inputBindings`).

id.description: Stable row identifier (unique within the table). Preserved through projection so provenance, scenario coverage, and reviewer comments remain row-anchored across edits.

inputCells.description: FEL boolean predicates, one per declared input in declaration order. Each evaluates in row scope (inputs bound to guard `inputBindings`-resolved values). MUST evaluate to a boolean (K-053). An empty array means 'matches always' — the conventional default-row pattern.

items.description: Single FEL boolean predicate evaluated in the row scope for the corresponding declared input column; empty string is invalid—use an empty inputCells array on the row for unconditional match.

outputCells.description: FEL value expressions, one per declared output in declaration order. Each evaluates in row scope (same bindings as input cells) and produces a value of the type declared in the corresponding `outputs[*].type`.

items.description: Single FEL expression producing the output column's value for this row when the row matches; MUST type-check against the declared output column type.

priority.description: Used only when the enclosing DecisionTable's `hitPolicy = priority`. Lower number = higher priority. Ties among matched rows are a K-052 violation.

rationale.description: Reviewer-facing rationale for this row, preserved as authoring provenance alongside the table so policy staff can explain why a row exists without reverse-engineering FEL cells.
examples: FFY26 SNAP gross income test for one- to two-person households per agency handbook §4.2.
Default deny row when no higher-priority bracket matches.

DecisionTableGuard.description: Structured guard form referencing a top-level `decisionTables[*]` entry. Composes with `Transition.guard` (Kernel §4.5.1). Evaluates by binding the table's declared inputs to the values produced by `inputBindings[*]` (FEL expressions in the full transition context), running the table per its hit policy, and returning the selected row's `outputColumn` value (which MUST be boolean for transition-guard usage).

kind.description: Discriminant for the structured guard object form; MUST remain the literal token `decisionTable` so processors can branch without heuristics.

ref.description: Identifier of an entry in the document's top-level `decisionTables[]`. MUST resolve (K-051) to a table id; mismatch is a hard authoring error surfaced by lint and runtime.

outputColumn.description: Name of the output column to read for the guard's boolean result. MUST exist on the referenced table; for transition-guard usage MUST be `boolean`-typed (K-053).

inputBindings.description: Map from each table input name to a FEL expression evaluated in the full transition context. The result is bound into the row scope under the input's name. Every declared input of the referenced table MUST have a binding (K-051).

additionalProperties.description: FEL expression evaluated in the transition's outer context; its value is bound to the named table input inside each row's evaluation scope.

onNoMatch.description: Behavior when zero rows match (and after `unique`/`first`/`priority` short-circuits). `false` (default) treats the guard as `false` — the transition is skipped per §4.6. `fail` rejects per Kernel §4.6/§4.7 transition resolution. Use `fail` when the table is intended to be exhaustive and a no-match represents an authoring error worth halting.

x-lm.intent: Structured (table-form) guard that preserves DecisionTable authoring fidelity through the substrate — alternative to FEL-string guard

event.description: Trigger event for this transition. When omitted, the transition is anonymous: it does not match an external event but still participates in continuous-mode guard re-scan (Runtime Companion §10.3).

oneOf.description: FEL expression evaluated before the transition fires. When the guard returns true, the transition is selected; when it returns false, the transition is skipped. When the guard throws or evaluates to a non-boolean, the processor MUST reject per Kernel §4.6/§4.7 transition resolution. Guards are stateless — they read case state but MUST NOT mutate it. The compiled FEL AST drives lint analysis (K-012 syntax, K-017 cross-case-state reference, K-019 function-set, AI-058 boolean shape).

guard.description: Either a FEL expression (string form) or a structured decision-table reference (object form) per Kernel §4.5.1. The object form lets authors keep table-shaped decision logic as a first-class structure through the substrate without flattening to chained FEL. Round-tripping is lossless. Both forms produce a boolean used by §4.6 transition resolution; both are stateless.

x-lm.intent: Where every conditional logic decision lives — accepts FEL-string (highest-traffic FEL surface) or DecisionTableGuard (structured table form per §4.5.1), lint-checked for syntax / cross-state / function-set / boolean-shape / table-row-coverage / hit-policy correctness

items.description: Semantic tag string attaching governance and policy to this transition. Governance attaches via the `lifecycleHook` seam (ADR 0077 §10.4) by matching tags. Common tags: `determination`, `adverse-decision`, `appeal-grant`, `correction`, `rescission`.

actor.description: Actor identifier responsible for performing this transition. MUST match an entry in the workflow's `actors[].id` and MUST use the same lexical pattern as `actors[].id` (ASCII letter first, then letters, digits, `.`, `_`, `-`). Single-actor transitions name one actor; multi-actor (signature-gated) transitions use the `actors` array (lint WOS-SIG-COVER-001 enforces signer coverage). Load-bearing for authority decisions and signature workflow correctness.

x-lm.intent: Who acts on the transition — load-bearing for authority decisions, signature coverage (WOS-SIG-COVER-001), and audit traceability

actions.description: Ordered list of actions the processor MUST execute as part of this transition, after guard evaluation and before destination state entry. Action shape per Kernel §9.2. Actions execute atomically with the state change — failure rolls back per Kernel §9.5 compensation.

target.description: Identifier of the destination state. MUST match a key in the enclosing lifecycle's states (or a substate when nested). The processor MUST reject transitions whose `target` does not resolve.

TransitionEvent.description: Typed trigger for explicit event delivery on a lifecycle transition (Kernel §4.5). Exactly one of the five discriminated branches MUST match; processors resolve runtime process_event string names per kind-specific rules in §4.9.10 and companion join semantics in §4.8.

Contracts.$comment: Validation/guard contracts and capability contracts. Pairs with bindings[] per ADR 0080 governed output-commit pipeline.
Contracts.description: Named validation and capability contracts referenced by lifecycle transitions, output bindings, and the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080). Contracts compose with the `contractHook` seam (ADR 0077 §10.2). Concrete shape varies by binding kind; see kernel/spec.md §9.2 for the merged contract surface.

OutputBinding.description: Governed output-commit pipeline projection. Per ADR 0080: source surface + contractRef + projection map + writeScope + mutationSource + verificationLevel. Validated mutations + Facts-tier provenance emit deterministically.

on.$comment: Source surface: state id, transition id, capability id, signal name.
on.description: Source surface that triggers this binding. Identifier of a state, transition, capability, or signal whose completion projects mutations through the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080). The binding fires when the source surface emits its terminal event.

contractRef.description: URI of the contract that validates the projected mutation before commit. Per ADR 0080's six-surface pipeline, every binding MUST reference a contract; the validator gates the commit. Formspec contract recommended for case-data writes.

projection.description: Map of source-output keys to case-file field paths. Defines how the source surface's output projects into the case state. Concrete shape is binding-specific; for Formspec bindings, projection follows the Mapping spec.

items.description: Field-path expression naming a case-file location this binding is permitted to mutate. The processor MUST reject mutations outside writeScope at commit time. Per ADR 0080 D-2, writeScope is the load-bearing authorization claim.

mutationSource.description: Provenance origin of the projected mutation under the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080). Recorded in Facts-tier provenance (Kernel §8); load-bearing for audit traceability and rights-impacting decision review. The closed-set values describe canonical provenance origins; vendor-namespaced `x-` values cover origins outside the canonical set.

x-lm.intent: Where the mutation came from — load-bearing audit-traceability claim per ADR 0080 governed output-commit pipeline

oneOf.description: Vendor-namespaced mutation source identifier under the `x-` prefix per Kernel §10.6 (`extensions` seam). Used when none of the canonical mutation sources (agent-extracted, system-fetched, human-entered, human-corrected, computed, self-attested) describes the actual provenance.

verificationLevel.description: Strength of verification applied to the projected mutation under the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080). Higher levels increase audit weight and enable stricter governance gates (e.g., `authoritative` defeats lower-tier challenges in ADR 0066 amendment/supersession evaluation). The closed-set values describe canonical verification regimes; vendor-namespaced `x-` values cover bespoke regimes (e.g., FHE-attested, dual-control). Lint WOS-VER-LEVEL-001 warns when `agents[].fallbackChain` is declared without any `verificationLevel` on the workflow.

x-lm.intent: How strongly verified the mutation is — load-bearing for governance gating and fallback-chain integrity (lint WOS-VER-LEVEL-001)

oneOf.description: Vendor-namespaced verification level identifier under the `x-` prefix per Kernel §10.6 (`extensions` seam). Used when none of the canonical levels (independent, attested, corroborated, authoritative) describes the binding's verification regime.

tier.description: Minimum provenance tier emitted. Facts is kernel default. Higher tiers attach via provenanceLayer seam (ADR 0077).

Governance.description: Embedded due-process, review, and pipeline governance. Required for rights-impacting and safety-impacting impactLevel. Was wos-workflow-governance.schema.json plus wos-policy-parameters.schema.json plus wos-due-process.schema.json plus wos-assertion-gate.schema.json.

dueProcess.$comment: Notice, opportunity to be heard, appeal mechanism, adverse-decision policy.
dueProcess.description: Due-process policy block covering notice, explanation, appeal, counterfactual, and continuation-of-services requirements.

reviewProtocols.description: Review protocol declarations describing independence, sampling, sequencing, and scope for governed review tasks.

pipelines.description: Governance pipeline declarations that define named stages and assertions used to evaluate controlled decisions.

taskCatalog.description: Governance task catalog describing review, notice, escalation, and remediation tasks available to the workflow.

delegation.description: Delegation policy block defining who may delegate authority, who may receive it, and what scope limits apply.

holds.description: Hold policy block describing allowed hold types, resume triggers, timeout behavior, and tagged lifecycle states.

policyParameters.$comment: Temporal parameters, deadline config, business calendar refs.
policyParameters.description: Named policy-parameter values resolved by governance rules, notices, timers, and runtime policy checks.

escalationLevels.description: Ordered escalation levels used by timers, holds, review protocols, and unresolved governance work.

amendmentTaxonomy.description: Five-mode amendment taxonomy declared by this workflow's governance, per ADR-0066 D-1 + Q26 maximalist closed-five position. Closed enum -- vendor extensions are deliberately not permitted at this key so the lint that pairs each declared mode with its authorizing-act record category (correctionAuthorized, amendmentAuthorized, rescissionAuthorized, reinstated; supersession is governed at the WOS/kernel layer per kernel spec caseRelationships (S5.5), not as a property on this workflow document) stays exhaustive. Authoring this enum makes the lint cheaper to land in follow-up work; absence here means the workflow asserts no amendments are permitted.

items.description: Allowed determination-change taxonomy value used to classify corrections, amendments, supersessions, rescissions, and reinstatements.

x-lm.intent: Closed five-mode amendment taxonomy per ADR-0066 D-1 / Q26 maximalist; lint target for taxonomy-mode ↔ record-kind pairing

recordLifecycle.description: Respondent-facing record-lifecycle affordance configuration per ADR-0066 D-6.2 (absorbs ADR-0158): the labeled projection of the kernel five-mode taxonomy onto the respondent-facing surface. Sibling to `amendmentTaxonomy` -- the taxonomy declares which kernel modes are PERMITTED; `recordLifecycle` declares which respondent-facing affordances (`correctable` / `withdrawable` / `disputable`) are offered + their per-act configuration. Three affordances; `rectification` has no respondent affordance (issuer-authored per D-6.2 last column), `reinstatement` has no respondent affordance (issuer-authored recovery per D-1.5). FW-0034 / FW-0038 own the formspec-web rendering surface; this block is the WOS-side form-policy declaration.

x-lm.intent: Respondent-facing record-lifecycle affordance config per ADR-0066 D-6.2; pairs with `amendmentTaxonomy` (correctable.kernelMode -> taxonomy literal; withdrawable.postDeterminationIntent -> 'rescission'); FW-0034 design dependency, FW-0038 build dependency

RecordLifecyclePolicy.description: Container for the three respondent-facing record-lifecycle affordances per ADR-0066 D-6.2. Each affordance is optional; absence means the affordance is not offered to the respondent. The three blocks are independent -- a workflow MAY enable any subset. Composition rules with `amendmentTaxonomy` are documented per-property; conformance lint pairs taxonomy-mode declarations with respondent-affordance opt-ins.

correctable.description: Respondent-facing correction affordance per ADR-0066 D-6.2 `correct` row. Projects onto kernel mode `correction` (narrow declared-subset) or `amendment` (substantive overflow) per FW-0034 design §3.2. When `enabled: true`, `amendmentTaxonomy` MUST contain `"correction"` (or `"amendment"` for substantive overflow); enforced by spec, lint candidate.

withdrawable.description: Respondent-facing withdrawal affordance per ADR-0066 D-6.2 `withdraw` row. Resolves to two axis cells per case-lifecycle-state at emission time (D-6.1.1): pre-determination emits `TerminateInstanceRequest { terminationKind: applicant-withdrawn }` (applicant authority); post-determination emits intent + ISSUER decides whether to emit kernel `rescission` (issuer authority, not respondent). When `postDeterminationIntent` is configured, `amendmentTaxonomy` MUST contain `"rescission"` and `requiresIssuerAcceptance` MUST be true; enforced by spec, lint candidate.

disputable.description: Respondent-facing dispute affordance per ADR-0066 D-6.2 `dispute` row. NEW PRIMITIVE -- counter-attestation, NOT lifecycle state transition. The original record and signature remain valid and verifiable; the dispute adds a `response.dispute-attached` Respondent Ledger event. No `kernelMode` binding because dispute consumes no kernel mode (D-6.2 last column 'none -- Respondent Ledger counter-attestation').

CorrectableAffordance.description: Per-act configuration for the respondent `correct` affordance per ADR-0066 D-6.2 + FW-0034 design §6.3. Pairs with `amendmentTaxonomy` containing `"correction"` (or `"amendment"` for substantive overflow). When `enabled: true`, `correctableFieldSet` MUST be authored to bound which fields the respondent may correct -- author-time policy, not respondent-side inference (per FW-0034 design §3.2 rejection of field-graph-analysis-driven scope).

enabled.description: Whether the `correct` affordance is offered to the respondent on this workflow. When false, the affordance MUST NOT render in the respondent surface; the runtime MAY still accept correction-shaped substrate emissions from authorized non-respondent actors (e.g., `rectification` per D-6.2 issuer authorship row).

correctableFieldSet.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointers naming the strict subset of response fields the respondent MAY correct via this affordance. MUST be non-empty when `enabled` is true (enforced by allOf branch). Bounds the correction surface so corrections cannot become a backdoor amendment per ADR-0066 Open Question 2 (Correction field-set scope).

items.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer (e.g., `/applicant/email`, `/household/members/0/income`).

window.description: Optional window declaration bounding when the affordance is available. Mirrors the kernel/governance temporal-parameter convention; runtime resolves `closesAt` against the case timeline.

requiresEvidence.description: When true, the respondent MUST attach evidence (uploaded document, structured assertion, or other governance-defined artifact) to the correction submission. The runtime rejects correction events lacking the evidence binding.

requiresReason.description: When true, the respondent MUST supply a free-text reason for the correction. Default true (FW-0034 design §6.3 default). The reason text rides on the `ResponseCorrection` event payload and inherits the `reasonField.accessControl.class` declaration.

kernelMode.description: Routes the affordance to the `correction` kernel-mode literal in `amendmentTaxonomy` per ADR-0066 D-1. Substantive-overflow routing to `amendment` is form-domain policy at the runtime layer (not declared here -- the affordance always ROUTES to `correction` at substrate emission; substantive escalation is a downstream governance decision per FW-0034 design §3.2).

reasonField.description: Optional metadata governing how the correction `reason` text is treated. Carries an `accessControl.class` declaration per ADR-0074 §1 so safe-* and other audience-policy classes can compose with the lifecycle event payload (per FW-0049 composition). When absent, the reason text inherits the default audience policy of the workflow.

class.description: Audience-policy class token per ADR-0074 §1. Treated as an opaque string by WOS; resolves through the Formspec Access-Class Registry (e.g., `safe-address`, `safe-contact`, `protected`).

allOf.$comment: Spec MUST: when enabled, correctableFieldSet MUST be authored and non-empty -- bounds the correction surface so corrections cannot become backdoor amendments (ADR-0066 Open Question 2).

WithdrawableAffordance.description: Per-act configuration for the respondent `withdraw` affordance per ADR-0066 D-6.2 + FW-0034 design §6.3. The affordance has TWO emission shapes resolved at runtime per case-lifecycle-state (D-6.1.1 meta-dimension): pre-determination is a direct `TerminateInstanceRequest { terminationKind: applicant-withdrawn }` (applicant authority; no kernel mode consumed); post-determination emits `response.withdrawn` with `rescissionRequested: true` and the ISSUER decides whether to emit the kernel `rescission` event (issuer authority -- authority-ladder integrity per ADR-0066 D-6.7). The respondent runtime carries intent; the kernel-mode emission belongs to issuer governance.

enabled.description: Whether the `withdraw` affordance is offered to the respondent on this workflow. When false, the affordance MUST NOT render in the respondent surface.

window.description: Optional window declaration bounding when the affordance is available. Pre-determination withdrawals are unconditional in slice 1; post-determination intent emissions honor this window.

requiresReason.description: When true, the respondent MUST supply a free-text reason for the withdrawal. Default true (FW-0034 design §6.3 default).

preDeterminationKernelMode.description: Routes the pre-determination withdraw to a `TerminateInstanceRequest { terminationKind: applicant-withdrawn }` per `work-spec/specs/api/instance.md:131` -- the `TerminationKind` reserved literal. No kernel amendment-taxonomy mode is consumed at the pre-determination boundary; termination is its own first-class instance-API verb.

postDeterminationIntent.description: When configured, declares that respondents MAY signal a post-determination rescission intent. formspec-web emits `response.withdrawn` with `rescissionRequested: true`; the ISSUER decides whether the kernel `rescission` event fires (issuer authority per D-6.7). Absence means post-determination withdrawals are not offered through this affordance (respondents see the affordance disabled once the case reaches determination).

requiresIssuerAcceptance.description: MUST be true when `postDeterminationIntent` is configured. Encodes the authority-ladder discipline of D-6.7: the post-determination respondent emission is a REQUEST projected through issuer governance, never a respondent-authored kernel mode. Enforced by spec (allOf branch).

partyScope.description: Case-level multi-party withdraw composition per FW-0034 design §7.4. DISTINCT from FW-0050 §5.2 per-signature withdraw which is per-party always (substrate rule, no `partyScope` axis). Single-party workflows MAY omit; multi-party workflows SHOULD declare. Default `all-parties-must-agree`.

allOf.$comment: Spec MUST: when postDeterminationIntent is configured, requiresIssuerAcceptance MUST be true -- ADR-0066 D-6.7 authority-ladder integrity (respondents do not directly author kernel rescission events).

DisputableAffordance.description: Per-act configuration for the respondent `dispute` affordance per ADR-0066 D-6.2 + FW-0034 design §6.3. NEW PRIMITIVE -- counter-attestation, NOT lifecycle state transition. The original record and signature remain valid and verifiable; the dispute adds an EXT-5 `response.dispute-attached` Respondent Ledger event. NO `kernelMode` binding because dispute consumes no kernel mode -- treating dispute as withdrawal, invalidation, or amendment is rejected on the same grounds as the D-1 alternative-considered.

enabled.description: Whether the `dispute` affordance is offered on this workflow. When false, the affordance MUST NOT render in the respondent surface.

signerOnly.description: When true (default), only the actor who signed the response MAY emit a dispute; non-signer respondents (e.g., a filer per EXT-36) cannot. When false, any party in scope MAY dispute. Default true per FW-0034 design §6.3.

requiresReason.description: When true (default), the respondent MUST supply a free-text dispute statement. The statement text rides on the `response.dispute-attached` event payload and inherits the `statementField.accessControl.class` declaration.

statementField.description: Optional metadata governing how the dispute `statement` text is treated. Carries an `accessControl.class` declaration per ADR-0074 §1 so safe-* and other audience-policy classes can compose with the lifecycle event payload (per FW-0049 composition).

class.description: Audience-policy class token per ADR-0074 §1. Treated as an opaque string by WOS; resolves through the Formspec Access-Class Registry.

RecordLifecycleWindow.description: Window declaration bounding when a respondent-facing record-lifecycle affordance is available. The `closesAt` value is an open prose token: anchor literals (`determination`, `submission`, `issuance`, `never`) or anchor+duration tokens (`issuance+30d`, `submission+P14D`) are common shapes per FW-0034 design §6.3. Vocabulary is intentionally open in slice 1; the runtime resolves the token against the case timeline. Closing the vocabulary belongs to a follow-up row once deployment usage stabilizes.

closesAt.description: Open prose token naming the case-timeline anchor (and optional offset) at which this affordance closes. Conventional shapes: `determination`, `submission`, `issuance`, `never`, `issuance+30d`, `submission+P14D`. Runtime resolution is deployment-defined in slice 1.

AgentDeclaration.description: Was wos-ai-integration.schema.json AgentDeclaration plus wos-agent-config.schema.json plus wos-drift-monitor.schema.json. Inlined per ADR 0076.

id.$comment: MUST match an actor.id where actor.type=='agent'.
id.description: Stable agent identifier referenced by workflow actors, invoker bindings, provenance records, and governance rules.

modelIdentifier.description: Provider or deployment model identifier used for this agent, recorded so invocations and provenance can be tied to a concrete model family.

modelVersion.description: Provider or deployment model version used for this agent, recorded so behavior can be audited across upgrades and recalibrations.

autonomy.$comment: Capped by impactLevel. rights-impacting MUST NOT exceed assistive without explicit governance permission.

deonticConstraints.description: Per-agent deontic constraints. Each constraint binds the agent (or a specified sub-actor) to a permission, prohibition, obligation, or right per OASIS LegalRuleML §3. The kernel checks `kind` and `expression` (a FEL string); the optional `actor`, `condition`, `defeasible`, `citationRefs`, and `x-legalruleml-iri` surfaces let governance reviewers carry the legal grounding without re-encoding it elsewhere.

confidenceFloor.description: Minimum calibrated confidence the agent must meet before its output may be used at the configured autonomy level.

confidenceDecay.description: Policy describing how an agent confidence score decays over time or across drift signals before recalibration or human review is required.

items.description: Agent identifier in the ordered fallback chain used when this agent cannot act, fails validation, or is demoted by governance policy.

fallbackChain.$comment: Actor ids, in order. MUST terminate in a human-review actor for rights-impacting transitions.

capabilities.$comment: Capability sub-shape: declared write scope, contract refs, tool grants.
capabilities.description: Capabilities this agent may perform, such as extraction, classification, drafting, or tool invocation; runtime policy evaluates requests against this list.

driftMonitoring.description: Agent-specific drift monitoring configuration, including observed signals and the governance response when drift is detected.

invoker.description: Substrate adapter discriminator (ADR 0064). Selects which AgentInvoker implementation handles invocations of this agent at runtime. Each `kind` corresponds to one adapter crate (`wos-agent-anthropic`, `wos-agent-mcp`, `wos-agent-a2a`, `wos-agent-http`, `wos-agent-stub`, `wos-agent-sdk-claude`). Deployment binds the discriminator to a concrete adapter; the workflow stays substrate-portable.

InvokerSpec.description: Tagged union over substrate adapters. Each `kind` value selects one wos-agent-* adapter crate per ADR 0064.

model.description: Anthropic model identifier used for the invocation, recorded so behavior can be audited by model family and release.

configRef.description: Optional Anthropic adapter configuration reference resolved by deployment tooling before invoking the selected model.
Claude Agent SDK configuration reference resolved by deployment tooling before starting the agent session.

server.description: MCP server identifier resolved at deployment time before dispatching the configured tool invocation.

tool.description: Tool name on the selected MCP server that the agent is allowed to call for this capability.

orchestrator.description: External orchestrator identifier resolved at deployment time before dispatching the agent capability request.

endpoint.description: HTTP endpoint URI used by the invoker when dispatching the agent capability request over HTTP.

schemaRef.description: Optional URI to OpenAPI / JSON Schema describing the contract.

oneOf.title: stub
oneOf.description: Deterministic in-memory stub used by conformance fixtures (`wos-agent-stub`).

responses.description: Canned responses streamed in declaration order per (capability_id, invocation_index).

capabilityId.description: Capability id this response satisfies. When omitted, matches any capability.

output.description: Output payload returned by the invoker.

confidence.description: Confidence score returned with the stubbed agent response; omitted values default to full confidence in tests.

citations.description: Citation array returned with the stubbed agent response and copied into invocation output for tests.

AIOversight.description: Cross-cutting AI policy. Disclosure, drift detection, narrative provenance, volume constraints. EU AI Act Art. 13 / OMB M-24-10 alignment.

disclosure.description: Disclosure policy for AI assistance in the workflow, including when people must be told that an agent contributed to a task, recommendation, or determination.

driftDetection.description: Workflow-level drift detection settings for AI agents, including monitoring cadence, recalibration triggers, and demotion behavior when model quality changes.

volumeConstraints.description: Workflow-level throughput limits for autonomous or assisted AI work, used to cap volume and force human review when operating limits are exceeded.

narrativeTier.description: Narrative authority tier for AI-generated explanations, distinguishing assistive narrative from authoritative reasoning used in rights-impacting decisions.

Signature.description: Signature content embedded in a $wosWorkflow document (Kernel §13 / T4 Signature Profile). Carries roles, signable documents, signing flow, evidence requirements, authentication policies, and policy hooks (reminders, expiry, decline, void, reassignment). Spec authority: `wos-runtime/src/runtime/signature.rs::SignatureProfileDocument`.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URI annotation for the embedded signature block; workflow identity remains on the enclosing envelope.

description.description: Optional human-readable note describing the embedded signature flow. Identity (url, version) is governed by the enclosing $wosWorkflow envelope per ADR 0063 §2.1; legacy `targetWorkflow` / `version` / `title` properties are retired.
examples: Applicant and eligibility worker signatures for a benefits determination packet.

roles.description: Signature role declarations binding signing roles to kernel actors.

id.description: Stable signing-role identifier referenced by signing steps, completion rules, and audit evidence.

role.description: Human-readable role label shown to signers and recorded in signature audit evidence.

actorId.description: Workflow actor identifier bound to this signing role when the role is assigned to a specific actor.

required.description: Whether this signing role is required before the signature flow can reach completion.

authenticationPolicyKey.description: Authentication-policy key that this signing role must satisfy before completing a signature step.

description.description: Human-readable description of the signing role and its responsibility in the signature flow.
examples: Caseworker responsible for affirming the reviewed benefits determination.

id.description: Stable signable-document identifier referenced by signing steps, evidence records, and custody bindings.

documentRef.description: Reference to the signable document artifact that the signer is affirming.

documentHash.description: Digest value for the signable document, computed with documentHashAlgorithm and bound into signature evidence.

documentHashAlgorithm.description: Hash algorithm used to compute documentHash for this signable document. Core admits sha-256; vendor algorithms must use the x-* extension namespace.

oneOf.description: Vendor-defined hash algorithm token. Vendor algorithm names MUST use the `x-*` extension namespace so core processors never confuse them with WOS-standard hash algorithms.

renderingRef.description: Reference to the rendering artifact or template used to produce the signable document view.

sourceResponseRef.description: Reference to the source response or evidence artifact whose rendered content or data is bound into the signable document.

signingFlow.description: Signing flow declaration: sequential, parallel, routed, or free-for-all.

id.description: Stable signing-step identifier referenced by dependencies, decline paths, expiry paths, and evidence records.

roleId.description: Signing-role identifier responsible for completing this signing step.

documentId.description: Signable-document identifier this signing step asks the signer to affirm.

signingIntent.description: Expected WOS signing-intent URI for this signing step. When present, runtime admission requires consumed source signature evidence to match exactly.

guard.description: FEL guard expression evaluated to decide whether this routed signing step is required for the case.

items.description: Signing-step identifier that must complete before this step becomes available.

required.description: Whether this signing step is required when its guard and dependencies are satisfied.

type.description: Completion rule type that determines whether all required signatures, any required signature, a counted threshold, or a named role set is required. Canonical values are 'all-required', 'any-required', 'count', and 'role-set'.

count.description: Number of completed signatures required when the completion rule uses a count-based threshold.

items.description: Signing-role identifier that must be completed when the completion rule names explicit roles.

evidence.description: Signature evidence requirements emitted into SignatureAffirmation provenance.

items.description: Required evidence field name that must be present before signature affirmation evidence is complete.

consentTextRef.description: Reference to the exact consent text shown to the signer before affirmation.

consentVersion.description: Version identifier for the consent text shown to the signer before affirmation.

acceptedAtPath.description: JSON path or case-file path where the signer acceptance timestamp is recorded for consent evidence.

affirmationPath.description: JSON path or case-file path where the signer affirmation value is recorded for consent evidence.

method.description: Canonical authentication method recorded in signature evidence, or an x-* vendor token.

oneOf.description: WOS-standard authentication method recorded for signature evidence and compared against the signature profile's authentication policy.
Vendor-defined authentication method token. Vendor methods MUST use the `x-*` namespace so profile policy matching remains explicit.

assuranceLevel.description: Identity-assurance level for the signer binding. Accepts NIST-style `ial1`–`ial3` / `aal1`–`aal3`, ranked `low`–`very-high`, or vendor `x-*` tokens.

oneOf.description: NIST SP 800-63-style subscriber and authenticator assurance labels (IAL/AAL) for identity evidence in signature audit.
Ranked assurance labels aligned with governance authority claims elsewhere in this schema for cross-tooling comparison.

Vendor-defined assurance discriminator using the `x-` extension namespace (ADR 0077). Processors MUST ignore unknown vendor tokens for forward compatibility. Conformance runtimes do not impose an ordinal rank between `x-*` assurance values and IAL/AAL or ranked tiers; they still enforce `method` match and `requiresInPerson` against declared authentication policies.

providerRef.description: Identity provider or credential service reference recorded with the signer identity binding.

externalAttestationRef.description: Reference to an external identity attestation that supports the signer identity binding.

custodyHookEligible.description: Whether signature evidence produced under this profile is eligible for custody-hook anchoring.

authenticationPolicies.description: Authentication policy declarations referenced by role.authenticationPolicyKey.

key.description: Stable authentication-policy key referenced by signature roles and signing steps.

method.description: Canonical authentication method required by this policy, or an x-* vendor token.

oneOf.description: WOS-standard authentication method required by this signature authentication policy.
Vendor-defined authentication method required by this policy. Vendor methods MUST use the `x-*` namespace and are matched literally by the runtime.

assuranceLevel.description: Required identity-assurance level before a signer may affirm. Same union as `evidence.identityBinding.assuranceLevel`: IAL/AAL, ranked labels, or `x-*` vendor tokens.

oneOf.description: NIST SP 800-63-style subscriber and authenticator assurance labels (IAL/AAL) for identity evidence in signature audit.
Ranked assurance labels aligned with governance authority claims elsewhere in this schema for cross-tooling comparison.

Vendor-defined assurance discriminator using the `x-` extension namespace (ADR 0077). Processors MUST ignore unknown vendor tokens for forward compatibility. Conformance runtimes do not impose an ordinal rank between `x-*` assurance values and IAL/AAL or ranked tiers; they still enforce `method` match and `requiresInPerson` against this policy.

providerRef.description: Reference to the identity provider, notary system, or credential service used to satisfy this authentication policy.

requiresInPerson.description: Whether the authentication policy requires in-person verification before the signer may complete the step.

requiresCredentialEvidence.description: Whether the signer must produce credential evidence before the authentication policy is considered satisfied.

reminders.description: Reminder policy emitting timer-driven events while signing is pending.

eventName.description: Runtime event name emitted when a signature reminder is due under this reminder policy.

items.description: ISO-8601 duration offset defining when a signature reminder should be sent.

schedule.description: ISO 8601 durations relative to signing-flow start.

templateKey.description: Notification template key used to render signature reminder notices.

eventName.description: Runtime event name emitted when a signature request reaches its configured expiry deadline.

after.description: ISO-8601 duration after which an incomplete signature request expires and follows the expiry path.

transitionId.description: Transition identifier fired when a signature request expires and the workflow follows the expiry path.
Transition identifier fired when a signer declines and the signature workflow follows the decline path.

reasonRequired.description: Whether a signer must provide a structured reason when declining a requested signature step.

items.description: Actor identifier authorized to reassign pending signature tasks under this policy.

accountability.description: Accountability mode recorded when a pending signature task is reassigned to another authorized actor.

reasonRequired.description: Whether a structured reason is required before a pending signature task may be reassigned.

auditCertificate.description: Audit certificate emission policy. Captures what the certificate carries when issued.

format.description: Audit certificate format emitted when signature evidence is summarized for downstream verification or export. Closed vocabulary for formats remains spec-gated; this leaf records the wire token honestly as an open identifier-shaped label until the signature profile spec ratifies the allowed set.

includeActorMetadata.description: Whether generated signature audit certificates include actor identifiers, roles, and authentication context.

includeEvidenceLinks.description: Whether generated signature audit certificates include references to supporting evidence and custody records.

signingMode.description: Signing mode summarized in the audit certificate, using the existing signing-flow type values.

voidPolicy.description: Void policy declaring who may void the signing flow and whether a reason is required.

items.description: Actor identifier authorized to void an in-progress or completed signature package under this policy.

reasonRequired.description: Whether a structured reason is required before the signature package may be voided.

deploymentLocalSigningIntents.description: Allowlist of deployment-local signing-intent URIs admitted by this workflow in addition to the §2.11.1 registered WOS set. Each URI MUST NOT use the `urn:wos:signing-intent:*` namespace (reserved for the WOS-registered set). **SUNSET NOTICE (2026-05-08):** Superseded by PostureDeclaration.signaturePolicy.allowedSigningIntents (ADR-0090). Will be removed in a future schema version after transition. Runtime admits a SignatureAffirmation whose `signingIntent` is registered OR present in this list; otherwise rejects fail-closed (Signature Profile §2.11.2).

items.description: Deployment-scoped signing-intent URI admitted by this workflow. MUST NOT use the reserved `urn:wos:signing-intent:*` namespace.

x-lm.intent: Deployment-local signing-intent allowlist — bridge to Posture Declaration (PLN-0384); admits non-WOS-namespace URIs; schema rejects urn:wos:signing-intent:* shadowing

posturePolicy.description: Reference to the deployment's Posture Declaration (ADR-0090). Supersedes deploymentLocalSigningIntents.

url.description: URI where the deployment Posture Declaration document is published or otherwise retrievable.

version.description: Optional immutable version identifier for the deployment Posture Declaration that governed signature admission.

x-lm.intent: Per-deployment posture declaration governing signature admission policy.

Custody.description: Embedded Trellis custody declaration. Load-bearing whenever a workflow claims anchoring on transitions or signature events. Was wos-custody-hook-encoding.schema.json (sidecar); promoted to embedded per ADR 0076 D-2 / Q4.

trustProfileRef.description: URI reference to the Trellis trust profile governing custody anchors, evidence binding, and verification expectations.

on.$comment: Transition id, signature event id, or determination tag.
on.description: Lifecycle event or custody milestone that triggers anchoring of the specified workflow evidence into the named ledger.

trellisLedger.description: Trellis ledger identifier that receives the custody anchor for this requirement when the trigger condition occurs.

exportBundleRef.description: URI reference to the export bundle profile that defines how workflow evidence is packaged for external custody transfer.

Advanced.description: Optional. Constraint zones (DCR), equity guardrails, SMT verifiable constraints, multi-step session config, circuit breakers. Was wos-advanced.schema.json plus wos-equity.schema.json plus wos-verification-report.schema.json (author-time half).

constraintZones.$comment: DCR constraint zones. Renamed from dcrZones for canonical naming.
constraintZones.description: Named constraint zones that group states, actors, tools, or transitions under shared runtime restrictions and governance checks.

equityGuardrails.$comment: Bias caps, disparity thresholds, equity FEL expressions. Renamed from equity for canonical naming.
equityGuardrails.description: Equity guardrail configuration used to evaluate outcome predicates, cohort checks, and remediation requirements before governed actions proceed.

verifiableConstraints.$comment: SMT constraint declarations. Verification report is a runtime artifact in wos-provenance-log.
verifiableConstraints.description: Verifiable constraint declarations that bind governed workflow behavior to machine-checkable evidence, proofs, or runtime attestations.

circuitBreaker.description: Circuit-breaker policy for stopping or degrading workflow automation when configured risk, error-rate, or external-dependency thresholds are crossed.

shadowMode.description: Shadow-mode configuration for evaluating a policy or automation path without making it authoritative for the live workflow outcome.

multiStepSessions.description: Multi-step session definitions that govern ordered agent reasoning sequences with checkpoints and human intervention points. Recovered from pre-ADR-0076 wos-advanced.schema.json.

toolGovernance.description: Tool governance configuration: permitted tools, side-effect policy, rate limits. Recovered from pre-ADR-0076 wos-advanced.schema.json.

agentLifecycle.description: Agent lifecycle state machine: initial state, transitions, degraded autonomy cap, suspension notifications. Recovered from pre-ADR-0076 wos-advanced.schema.json.

calibration.description: Confidence calibration method and retraining cadence. Recovered from pre-ADR-0076 wos-advanced.schema.json.

driftDetection.description: Statistical drift detection methods and thresholds for agent output monitoring. Recovered from pre-ADR-0076 wos-advanced.schema.json.

Assurance.description: Optional. Assurance level declaration, attestation, subject continuity. Was wos-assurance.schema.json.

attestation.description: Assurance attestation policy describing what evidence must exist before an identity, authority, or continuity claim is accepted.

subjectContinuity.description: Subject-continuity rules that bind a person, actor, or case subject across identity events and assurance upgrades.

FactsTierRecord.$comment: Composition: top-level event enum selects which payload fields apply; per-kind MUSTs are expressed via allOf conjunct $refs at the provenance log item layer. Do not add conflicting const event on sibling conjunct arms (globally unsatisfiable instances). A oneOf-per-record-kind layout at the log item layer is a separate ADR-level decision, not a silent schema tweak.
FactsTierRecord.description: A persisted WOS provenance record emitted by runtime execution. Facts-tier semantics are normative in the kernel; higher tiers reuse the same envelope and advertise their tier through `auditLayer`. State transition records whose transitionTags include 'determination' MUST include a caseFileSnapshot so later notices, appeals, and override reviews can reproduce the exact factual basis used by the decision.

not.description: D26 removed the inner recordKind discriminator; Facts-tier records dispatch by required event literal.

id.description: TypeID-structured identifier for this facts-tier provenance record. Reserved family prefix: `prov`.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp stamped by the runtime append path before persistence.

actorId.description: Identifier of the actor who triggered the record, when the processor can name one.

fromState.description: Kernel lifecycle state label naming the configuration leaf immediately before this transition-oriented provenance record was emitted.

toState.description: Kernel lifecycle state label naming the configuration leaf immediately after this transition-oriented provenance record was emitted.

event.description: Triggering event or reserved event literal carried by the record category.

data.description: Arbitrary JSON object carrying record-kind-specific correlation keys, diagnostics, or structured details not represented by top-level scalar provenance fields.

auditLayer.description: Audit tier for the persisted/exported record. Facts-tier records are normative in the kernel; higher layers reuse the same envelope and set a different tier literal.

actorType.description: Actor classification when the processor can resolve one canonically. Reserved literals are `human`, `system`, and `agent`; extension values MUST use an `x-` prefix.

lifecycleState.description: Lifecycle state at the time of the action when the processor can determine it canonically.

definitionVersion.description: Version of the governing Kernel Document stamped at append time.

inputs.description: Input entity references used by this activity.

items.description: Opaque or resolvable token naming an input entity this activity consumed, aligned with PROV-O `prov:used` semantics for downstream audit graph materialization.

outputs.description: Output entity references generated by this activity.

items.description: Opaque or resolvable token naming an output entity this activity produced so exporters can stitch forward references into case files or external systems.

transitionTags.description: Semantic tags copied from the firing transition when this record represents a state transition. The presence of 'determination' makes caseFileSnapshot mandatory.

items.description: One semantic transition tag copied from the Kernel transition definition, such as 'determination' for consequential decisions or 'review' for human review steps.

mutationSource.description: Origin of the mutation when event is 'caseStateMutation'. OPTIONAL on other record categorys. Reserved literals trace the provenance chain from human entry through AI extraction to system-fetch and computed derivation.

verificationLevel.description: OPTIONAL verification strength of this mutation. When present, signals the degree of independent confirmation behind the written value. Tying this to 'determination'-tagged transitions is policy-shaped (governance profile), not a blanket kernel MUST.

canonicalEventHash.description: Trellis `canonical_event_hash` text stamped after custody admission, when present. Current Trellis service receipts use `sha256:<lowercase hex>`; older local fixtures may use bare lowercase hex.

D26 agreement guard target for intakeAccepted facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for noteAdded facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the flat Facts-tier event shape.
D26 agreement guard target for keyRebind facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for intakeRejected facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for intakeDeferred facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for forEachIterationStarted facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for forEachIterationCompleted facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for forEachCompleted facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for signatureAffirmation facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for signatureAdmissionFailed facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for determinationRescinded facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for reinstated facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for clockStarted facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.

D26 agreement guard target for identityAttestation facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for stateTransition facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.

transitionEvent.description: Kernel workflow event that triggered the transition; distinct from the canonical D26 record event literal.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target for capabilityInvocation facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for correctionAuthorized facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
D26 agreement guard target for amendmentAuthorized facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.

D26 agreement guard target for authorizationAttestation facts records. FactsTierRecord requires event after D26; this guard dispatches the typed payload shape.
